title: Santalodes bakeri
taxonomy: Santalodes bakeri is classified under the family Santalaceae, commonly known as the sandalwood or mistletoe family. This family consists of approximately 900 species of parasitic or hemiparasitic plants. Santalodes bakeri is a genus within the family, containing a single species that is native to Australia. The classification of Santalodes bakeri is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics as a flowering plant with parasitic tendencies.

intro: Santalodes bakeri is a species of sandalwood tree, native to New Caledonia, characterized by its fragrant and durable wood. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Santalodes bakeri include sesquiterpenes, including santalols and santalenes, which contribute to its medicinal and fragrant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Santalodes bakeri is often used to make essential oils, extracts, and other preparations, such as incense sticks and wood chips, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
